$comment: ADOS Reference Model Bank — Archetype records (ADOS-EVAL-REFERENCE-MODEL-BANK). An Archetype answers 'what reusable KIND of thing is being delivered' (ADOS-EVAL-ARCHETYPE). It is NOT a Domain, a file extension, or a tool stack. Each item gets exactly one primary_archetype; secondary archetypes are added only for distinct material contribution (ADOS-EVAL-SECONDARY-ARCHETYPE). 'field_contract' lists the archetype-conditioned keys the summarizer must fill in archetype_fields; each key maps to a one-line meaning. 'signals' are deterministic hints used by classify.py to form a prior — they justify a candidate, they do not decide it.
drift_guards: Archetype must not mean Domain (ADOS-EVAL-DOMAIN), a file extension, or a tool stack.
Do not choose the Primary Archetype from the most visible single file (ADOS-EVAL-PRIMARY-ARCHETYPE drift guard).
Incidental code in a transcript does not make the item 'software_app'; ask what is being DELIVERED.
A Secondary Archetype must earn its place through distinct material pressure, not decoration.

archetypes.label: User-facing application
archetypes.when_to_use: A runnable user-facing product (web/CLI/desktop/mobile) is the accepted Delivery and acceptance turns on its behavior/workflow.

field_contract.how_to_update: How to move it to the next version.

archetypes.label: Portable runtime / library package
archetypes.when_to_use: A packaged, reusable runtime, library, or module consumed by other software rather than an end user UI.

field_contract.api_surface: array: key entry points / exported functions.

archetypes.label: AI agent / prompt / skill
archetypes.when_to_use: The Delivery is a prompt, system message, agent definition, skill, or LLM behavior contract.
signals: discussion of model behavior, tools, guardrails

field_contract.role_or_persona: What the agent/prompt is meant to be or do.
field_contract.instructions_summary: Core behavior contract in one paragraph.
field_contract.how_to_use: How to invoke / deploy the prompt or agent.

archetypes.label: Automation / diagnostic script
archetypes.when_to_use: A script/tool that automates a task or diagnoses a system; value is the operation it performs, not a UI.

field_contract.what_it_automates: The task or check it performs.

archetypes.label: Data extraction / processing pipeline
archetypes.when_to_use: The Delivery transforms, extracts, parses, or analyzes data into a structured output.

archetypes.label: Research / analysis project
archetypes.when_to_use: A durable investigation producing findings, comparisons, or recommendations grounded in evidence.

field_contract.methods: array: how the investigation was conducted.

archetypes.label: Study / education resource
archetypes.when_to_use: Material whose purpose is learning, tutoring, practice, or test preparation.

field_contract.learning_outcome: What the learner should be able to do.

archetypes.label: Content / writing deliverable
archetypes.when_to_use: The Delivery is written content: copy, essays, emails, documentation prose, translations.

field_contract.content_type: What kind of writing (email, essay, post, doc).

archetypes.label: Media / image generation
archetypes.when_to_use: The Delivery is generated or edited media (images, audio, video).

archetypes.label: Advisory / troubleshooting
archetypes.when_to_use: A diagnostic Q&A resolving a concrete problem (device, config, health, legal, etc.) with advice or a fix.
signals: error message / device / 'why won't this work'

field_contract.resolution: The advice or fix arrived at.

archetypes.label: Personal administration / planning
archetypes.when_to_use: Help with a personal/logistical task: scheduling, messages, planning, decisions, life admin.

field_contract.task: What the user was trying to get done.
field_contract.outcome: The decision, plan, or message produced.

archetypes.label: Knowledge Q&A / explanation
archetypes.when_to_use: A factual or conceptual question answered with an explanation; no durable artifact.

archetypes.label: Controlled spec / schema / glossary
archetypes.when_to_use: The Delivery is a governed specification: a JSON schema, controlled vocabulary, ontology, standard, or contract document.
